name: The Brain Engine
oracle_text:
Whenever a nontoken creature you control dies, exile it with a charge counter on ~.
{2}{B}, {T}, Remove three charge counters from ~: Return target creature card from exile to the battlefield under your control. It's a black Zombie in addition to its other colors and types.
flavor_text: Feretha's body sat on the throne. His brain hung in the basement, dreaming of a city it no longer controlled.
design_notes: A legendary enchantment artifact representing the machine keeping Feretha's brain alive — and the broader super-science of brain extraction in the setting. It stockpiles dead creatures (exiling them to track via charge counters) then reanimates from exile, not graveyard, which is a distinctive twist. Requiring 3 charge counters means you need three creatures to die before you get one back — this is a slow engine, not a combo piece. The Zombie typing adds black flavor. The tap requirement means one activation per turn. At 5 mana with a 3-death investment before payoff, this is powerful but appropriately slow for a rare build-around. Raise dead from exile is black's reanimation wheelhouse.
